title: Joint Probability Distribution for Two Discrete Random Variables (X and Y)
description: A grid-based visualization showing all possible combinations of outcomes for two discrete random variables, with each cell's size and color intensity representing the joint probability P(X=x, Y=y).
elements: Random Variable X Axis (with outcomes x1, x2, ...)
Random Variable Y Axis (with outcomes y1, y2, ...)
Grid Cells (each representing a unique (x_i, y_j) pair)
Probability Value Label inside each cell
Marginal Probability Sums on the axes
relationships: Each grid cell is at the intersection of an outcome from X and an outcome from Y
The value in a cell is the probability of that joint outcome occurring
The sum of probabilities in a row represents the marginal probability P(Y=y_j)
The sum of probabilities in a column represents the marginal probability P(X=x_i)
The sum of all cell probabilities equals 1
